attributes.name: ASPXSpy Webshell Deployment - Known Filenames
attributes.description: Detects creation of .aspx files with known ASPXSpy webshell filenames used by Agrius/Pink Sandstorm. Common variants include aspxspy.aspx, error5.aspx, and contact.aspx deployed to establish initial access on compromised web servers.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "aspx" and (file.name in ("aspxspy.aspx", "error5.aspx", "contact.aspx", "shell.aspx", "cmd.aspx", "webshell.aspx", "tunnel.aspx", "proxy.aspx", "upload.aspx", "eval.aspx") or (not file.path : ("*\\inetpub\\*", "*\\wwwroot\\*", "*\\Program Files\\*", "*\\Exchange Server\\*")))
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

attributes.name: Windows Service Creation with Non-Standard Binary Path
attributes.description: Detects sc.exe creating Windows services with binPath pointing to non-standard directories such as user-writable paths, temp directories, or staging locations. Agrius uses IPsec Helper service name as cover for persistence. This is a common persistence technique across many threat actors.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "sc.exe" and process.args : "create" and process.args : ("binPath*", "binpath*") and not process.args : ("C:\\Program Files*", "C:\\Windows\\System32*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64*") and process.args : ("C:\\Users*", "C:\\ProgramData*", "C:\\Windows\\Temp*", "*\\AppData\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate software installers creating services from ProgramData

attributes.name: Kernel Driver Service Creation via sc.exe - BYOVD Indicator
attributes.description: Detects sc.exe creating kernel-type services, which is the mechanism used in Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver (BYOVD) attacks. Agrius deploys GMER64.sys via this method to disable EDR at the kernel level. Legitimate kernel driver installations are performed by system processes, not user-initiated sc.exe commands.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "sc.exe" and process.args : "create" and process.args : "kernel" and not process.parent.name in ("services.exe", "msiexec.exe", "DrvInst.exe", "TiWorker.exe", "setup.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate driver installation by system administrators using sc.exe

attributes.name: EDR/AV Service Tampering via sc.exe
attributes.description: Detects attempts to disable, stop, or delete security product services using sc.exe. Agrius targets 11+ EDR/AV vendors including Microsoft Defender, CrowdStrike, SentinelOne, Cortex XDR, Carbon Black, Sophos, ESET, Trend Micro, Elastic, Cylance, and Symantec. This is a critical defense evasion technique that must be detected immediately.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "sc.exe" and process.args in ("config", "stop", "delete") and process.args in ("Sense", "WinDefend", "MsSense", "CSFalconService", "SentinelAgent", "SentinelStaticEngine", "CortexXDR", "cyserver", "CbDefense", "CbDefenseWSC", "SAVService", "ekrn", "EsetService", "Ntrtscan", "ds_agent", "elastic-endpoint", "CylanceSvc", "SepMasterService", "ccSvcHst", "MpDefenderCoreService")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Authorized security tool maintenance during change windows

attributes.name: GMER64.sys Driver File Creation
attributes.description: Detects creation of GMER64.sys driver files outside of standard Windows driver directories. GMER is a legitimate anti-rootkit tool that is frequently abused by threat actors including Agrius for Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver (BYOVD) attacks to disable EDR at the kernel level.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.name : ("GMER64.sys", "GMER.sys", "gmer64.sys", "gmer.sys") and not file.path : ("*\\System32\\drivers\\*", "*\\DriverStore\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate use of GMER anti-rootkit tool by IT security staff

attributes.name: Mass File Modification - Potential Wiper Activity
attributes.description: Detects a single process performing rapid bulk file modifications, which is characteristic of wiper malware such as Agrius MultiLayer, PartialWasher, or BFG Agonizer. Threshold is 20+ file modifications within a 2-minute window from a single initiating process.
attributes.query: event.category:file AND event.action:(modification OR deletion) AND NOT process.name:(svchost.exe OR SearchIndexer.exe OR MsMpEng.exe OR TiWorker.exe OR msiexec.exe)
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Build systems with extensive file I/O

attributes.name: Windows Event Log Clearing via wevtutil
attributes.description: Detects the use of wevtutil.exe to clear Windows Event Logs, a critical anti-forensics technique used by Agrius and many other threat actors to destroy evidence of intrusion activities. Clearing Security, System, Application, or PowerShell logs is almost always malicious in enterprise environments.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "wevtutil.exe" and process.args : "cl" and process.args in ("Application", "Security", "System", "Windows PowerShell", "Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational", "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Scheduled log maintenance (should be extremely rare and change-managed)

attributes.name: Self-Deleting Batch Script with Ping Delay - Agrius Pattern
attributes.description: Detects execution of batch scripts that use ping-based delays followed by self-deletion, which is the signature anti-forensics pattern of Agrius remover.bat. This pattern is also used by other APT groups to clean up attack artifacts and delay execution to evade detection timing.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "cmd.exe" and process.args : "*.bat" and (process.args in ("remover.bat", "cleaner.bat", "deleter.bat", "wiper.bat") or process.command_line : "*del*%~f0*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Some legacy installer cleanup scripts that self-delete

attributes.name: Agrius Attack Chain - Multiple Destructive TTPs Correlated
attributes.description: Detects the Agrius multi-wiper attack chain by correlating three critical indicators on the same host within a 4-hour window: (1) service creation or modification via sc.exe, (2) EDR service tampering attempts, and (3) event log clearing via wevtutil. Observing all three on the same host is an extremely high-confidence indicator of a destructive campaign.
attributes.query: sequence by host.id with maxspan=4h [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "sc.exe" and process.args : "create"] [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "sc.exe" and process.args in ("config", "stop") and process.args in ("WinDefend", "Sense", "CSFalconService", "SentinelAgent", "CylanceSvc", "CbDefense", "ekrn", "SepMasterService", "CortexXDR", "elastic-endpoint")] [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "wevtutil.exe" and process.args : "cl"]
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Extremely unlikely outside of authorized security testing
